title: haemodoraceae
intro: The Haemodoraceae rank is a family of flowering plants. It is a group of about 100 species of plants commonly known as bloodflowers or kangaroo paws, which are native to Australia, New Zealand, and nearby islands. This family is characterized by its unique flowers, often with distinctive markings and shapes, and its ability to thrive in a variety of environments. The Haemodoraceae family is an important part of the flora in many regions, providing nectar-rich flowers and attractive foliage.
